item.title: Trump announces first-ever Republican midterm convention in Dallas
item.description: President Donald Trump has announced that Republicans will hold their first-ever national convention ahead of the midterm elections in Dallas.

item.title: Rep. Tom Kean reveals depression diagnosis as reason for months-long absence from Congress
item.description: New Jersey Republican Rep. Tom Kean was back on Capitol Hill Tuesday, where he announced his four-month, previously unexplained absence from Congress was due to depression. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Democratic Socialists of America co-chair on New York momentum, new test with Colorado House race
item.description: Democratic socialists are facing a test of power out west in the primary race for Colorado's First Congressional District. Ashik Siddique, Democratic Socialists of America co-chair, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: House GOP agenda stalls over holdouts' insistence on SAVE America Act
item.description: The holdouts blocked Speaker Mike Johnson's plan to merge the SAVE America Act with the annual defense policy bill before sending it over to the Senate.

item.title: Breaking down Supreme Court's birthright citizenship decision
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday affirmed a more than 100-year-old principle that if you're born in the U.S., you're an American citizen. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Jessica Levinson have more.

item.title: Trump calls Supreme Court ruling on transgender athletes a "BIG WIN"
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday praised the Supreme Court's decision allowing states to ban transgender athletes from competing in girls' and women's sports. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: This number helps explain why many Americans are down on the economy
item.description: American workers' share of the nation's income is at its lowest point in almost 80 years, as more of the economy's gains flow to corporations and investors.

item.title: Iran's top negotiator says country ready for war as peace talks inch along
item.description: A long-term U.S.-Iran peace deal may depend on a separate agreement between Israel and Lebanon. Analysts say that presents a problem.

item.title: New student loan rules take effect July 1. Here's what to know.
item.description: Loan revamp affects how much students and families can borrow to pay for college, as well as their repayment options.

item.title: Idaho AG says states without trans athlete bans will see "further litigation" after SCOTUS ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday ruled that states can ban transgender athletes from competing in girls' and women's sports. Idaho Attorney General Raúl Labrador, whose state has such a ban in place, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: New details on Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce's wedding events in New York City
item.description: After all the speculation, law enforcement sources are revealing new information about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding events this holiday weekend. CBS News' Anna Schecter reports.

item.title: NAACP general counsel lauds SCOTUS birthright citizenship ruling: "You can't write that right away"
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday struck down President Trump's executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship. Kristen Clarke, NAACP general counsel, joins "The Takeout" with her reaction.

item.title: Takeaways from Supreme Court decision upholding transgender bans in girls' and women's sports
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld state bans on transgender athletes in girls and women's sports on Tuesday. CBS News' Jared Hill reports and former U.S. gymnast Jennifer Sey joins CBS News with more.

item.title: Unpacking Supreme Court's transgender athlete ban and birthright citizenship decisions
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday upheld birthright citizenship following a challenge from President Trump. The court also allowed states to ban transgender athletes from girls' and women's sports. CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford has the details.

item.title: Mike Johnson says he is "disappointed" in Supreme Court's birthright citizenship ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday struck down an executive order from President Trump that sought to end birthright citizenship. House Speaker Mike Johnson called the decision disappointing. Andrea Flores, founder of Securing America's Promise, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Behind the growing tension between Trump and Netanyahu over the Iran war
item.description: The memorandum of understanding between the U.S. and Iran has led to signs of a very public rift between President Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. CBS News senior foreign correspondent Imtiaz Tab talks with Israeli journalist and author Yaakov Katz about what's behind the growing tension and what it could mean for both countries.

item.title: Crews in Venezuela working to rescue security guard trapped under rubble since earthquakes
item.description: Crews in La Guaira, Venezuela, are attempting to rescue a security guard who has been trapped under the rubble of his workplace since last week's back-to-back earthquakes. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the latest.

item.title: DOJ investigating Sen. Ruben Gallego's use of campaign funds, sources say
item.description: The probe focuses on Gallego's use of campaign funds for family trips, a source told CBS News. He has denied wrongdoing.

item.title: NASA astronauts operate on space station's broken robot arm
item.description: The orbital surgery on the International Space Station returned the Canadian-built robot arm to full health after its "wrist" joint failed last month.

item.title: LeBron James to leave Lakers, play elsewhere in 24th NBA season
item.description: James, 41, played eight seasons with the Lakers and led the franchise to its 17th NBA championship in the 2019-2020 season.

item.title: Taylor Swift's history with MSG, from talent competition to wedding bash
item.description: Taylor Swift has had a decadeslong history with Madison Square Garden, performing there as early as 2003 and even celebrating her 30th birthday at the venue.

item.title: How the Supreme Court ruled on Trump's agenda this term
item.description: The court's decisions cemented Mr. Trump's authority over vast swathes of the government, while delivering significant setbacks to his agenda in other areas.

item.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

item.title: Less than 40% of U.S. households can afford a starter home, study finds
item.description: The typical non-homeowner household earns about $7,000 less than what's needed to buy an entry-level home, according to LendingTree.

item.title: Supreme Court takes up challenges to AR-15 bans
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed to take up challenges to so-called assault-weapons bans in Cook County, Illinois, and Connecticut.

item.title: Maps show heat dome forecast to scorch major U.S. cities this week
item.description: A heat wave will blast much of the eastern U.S. this week, and forecasters say temperatures will feel even hotter because of the high humidity that's arriving with it.

item.title: D.C.'s July 4th fireworks will have "TSA-style" security
item.description: This year's Fourth of July celebrations in D.C. — marking the nation's 250th birthday — will include hours of military flyovers and a massive fireworks display that could stretch late into the night.

item.title: Here's what to know about Medicare's new GLP-1 weight-loss drug program
item.description: For the first time, Medicare will cover GLP-1 drugs prescribed solely for weight loss for eligible beneficiaries at a $50 monthly copay.

item.title: Poll: America at 250 and some of what Americans think is best about U.S.
item.description: Americans weighed in on what's best about the U.S., its greatest invention, most representative food and more in latest CBS News poll.

item.title: Aide who planned JFK Jr.'s secret wedding weighs in on Swift-Kelce rumors
item.description: RoseMarie Terenzio, who was JFK Jr.'s former chief of staff and planned his secret wedding to Carolyn Bessette, said she doesn't think Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are getting married at Madison Square Garden.
